sn4.19:1.2: Now at that time the Blessed One was instructing, urging, rousing, & encouraging the monks with a Dhamma talk concerning unbinding.
sn4.19:1.3: The monks—attentive, interested, lending ear, focusing their entire awareness—were listening to the Dhamma.
sn4.19:2.1: Then the thought occurred to Māra, the Evil One:
sn4.19:2.2: “Gotama the contemplative is instructing, urging, rousing, & encouraging the monks with a Dhamma talk concerning unbinding. The monks—attentive, interested, lending ear, focusing their entire awareness—are listening to the Dhamma.
sn4.19:2.3: What if I were to go to Gotama the contemplative to obscure his vision?”
sn4.19:2.4: Then Māra the Evil One, taking on the form of a farmer with a large plowshare over his shoulder, carrying a long goad stick—his hair disheveled, his clothes made of coarse hemp, his feet splattered with mud—went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, said,
sn4.19:2.5: “Hey, contemplative. Have you seen my oxen?”
sn4.19:2.6: “And what are your oxen, Evil One?”
sn4.19:2.7: “Mine alone is the eye, contemplative. Mine are forms, mine is the dimension of consciousness & contact at the eye.
sn4.19:2.8: Where can you go to escape me?
sn4.19:2.13: Mine alone is the intellect, contemplative. Mine are ideas, mine is the dimension of consciousness & contact at the intellect.
sn4.19:2.14: Where can you go to escape me?”
sn4.19:3.1: “Yours alone is the eye, Evil One. Yours are forms, yours is the sphere of consciousness & contact at the eye.
sn4.19:3.2: Where no eye exists, no forms exist, no dimension of consciousness & contact at the eye exists: There, Evil One, you cannot go.
sn4.19:3.9: Yours alone is the intellect, Evil One. Yours are ideas, yours is the dimension of consciousness & contact at the intellect.
sn4.19:3.10: Where no intellect exists, no ideas exist, no dimension of consciousness & contact at the intellect exists: There, Evil One, you cannot go.”
sn4.19:4.2: ‘This is mine’; and those who say,
sn4.19:4.4: contemplative, you can’t escape from me.”
sn4.19:5.1: The Buddha: “What they speak of isn’t mine,
sn4.19:5.2: and I’m not one of those who speak it.
sn4.19:5.4: You won’t even see my tracks.”
sn4.19:6.1: Then Māra the Evil One—sad & dejected at realizing, “The Blessed One knows me; the One Well-Gone knows me”—vanished right there.
